methods.method_name: Field Experiment Setup and Fertilization Application
methods.description: Process for establishing the field experiment site, dividing it into sub-blocks, assigning different fertilization variants (conventional mineral NPK and biomass ash), and applying the fertilizers before sowing winter oilseed rape.

steps.description: The field experiment was established in autumn 2018 in Korzenica (Podkarpackie Province, Jarosław County, GPS coordinates: 500.02'.16.3N, 220.55'.06.4E).
steps.searchable_summary: Establish field experiment site at specified location and time

steps.description: The experiment was set up in the arable layer (0-27 cm) of podzolic soil with the particle size distribution of loamy silt (Working Group WBR 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize soil type and particle size distribution at the experimental site

steps.description: In this field experiment, a compact strip was divided into 7 sub-blocks, each with an area of 162 m 2 , where different fertilization variants were applied.
steps.searchable_summary: Divide experimental area into 7 sub-blocks of 162 m² each

steps.description: Each variant in the experiment was applied in three replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign fertilization variants to sub-blocks with three replicates per variant

steps.description: A conventional mineral fertilizer NPK and an unconventional fertilizer, i.e., ash from combustion of biomass (willow Salix viminalis L.), were used in the experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and prepare conventional mineral NPK and biomass ash fertilizers for application

steps.description: The biomass ash doses were balanced to the amount of potassium introduced into the soil.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate biomass ash doses equivalent to specific potassium amounts

steps.description: In all experiment variants, constant nitrogen (81.3 kg N ha −1 ) and phosphorus (34 kg P ha −1 ) mineral fertilization were applied.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply constant mineral nitrogen (81.3 kg N ha⁻¹) and phosphorus (34 kg P ha⁻¹) fertilization to all experimental variants
outputs: Experimental plots with baseline N and P fertilization

steps.description: The fertilization of the winter rape plants at the following levels was the experimental factor: -Control-no K 2 O fertilization; -NPK K 2 O in mineral fertilizers (127 kg K 2 O ha −1 ); -100 kg K 2 O ha −1 in ash (0.5 t ha −1 of ash in bulk weight); -200 kg K 2 O ha −1 in ash (1.0 t ha −1 of ash in bulk weight); -300 kg K 2 O ha −1 in ash (1.5 t ha −1 of ash in bulk weight); -400 kg K 2 O ha −1 in ash (2.0 t ha −1 of ash in bulk weight); -500 kg K 2 O ha −1 in ash (2.5 t ha −1 of ash in bulk weight).
steps.searchable_summary: Define and apply specific potassium fertilization levels as the experimental factor
inputs: Defined K₂O levels (0, 127, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500 kg K₂O ha⁻¹)
outputs: Experimental plots with assigned and applied K₂O fertilization variants

steps.description: The fertilization was applied before sowing.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply all fertilizers to the experimental plots before sowing winter oilseed rape
outputs: Fertilized experimental plots ready for sowing

methods.description: Procedure for collecting soil samples at specific times and depths and determining their physicochemical properties including reaction (pH), electrolytic conductivity (EC), and moisture content.

steps.description: Soil was sampled at a depth of 0-5 cm from the arable layer before the experiment in the autumn of 2018...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect soil samples from 0-5 cm depth before the experiment starts

steps.description: ...and in each experimental year after plant harvest (at the end of August).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect soil samples from 0-5 cm depth after plant harvest each experimental year
inputs: Sampling time (end of August each year)

steps.description: The following physicochemical properties were determined in the soil samples after drying...
steps.searchable_summary: Dry collected soil samples

steps.description: ...and sieving through a sieve with a 2 mm mesh diameter.
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve dried soil samples through a 2 mm mesh

steps.description: Soil reaction was measured with the potentiometric method using an HI-4221 pH meter (Hanna Instruments, Nusfalau, Romania) at a soil-to-solution ratio of 1:2.5.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure soil pH using potentiometric method with 1:2.5 soil-to-solution ratio

steps.description: Soil electrolytic conductivity (EC), which is a measure of soil salinity, was determined with the conductometric method using an HI-2316 EC meter (Hanna Instruments, Nusfalau, Romania) at a soil-to-solution ratio of 1:5.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure soil electrolytic conductivity (EC) using conductometric method with 1:5 soil-to-solution ratio

steps.description: Soil moisture was measured with the drying-weighing method after soil sampling in Kopecki cylinders.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure soil moisture content using the drying-weighing method
inputs: Fresh soil samples (collected in Kopecki cylinders)

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the impact of biomass combustion ashes on selected physicochemical properties of the podzolic soil.

steps.description: The impact of the biomass combustion ashes on the selected physicochemical properties of the podzolic soil was analyzed using STATISTICA 13.3 software (StatSoft, Tulsa OK Oklahoma, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: Load and prepare soil physicochemical property data for statistical analysis in STATISTICA software

steps.description: One-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was performed...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) to test the effect of fertilization variants on soil properties
inputs: Soil properties (dependent variables: pH, EC, moisture)
outputs: ANOVA results (F-statistics, p-values, degrees of freedom)

steps.description: ...using the Tukey HSD multiple comparison test to identify homogeneous groups (p < 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Tukey HSD post-hoc test to identify statistically homogeneous groups among fertilization variants for each soil property
outputs: Tukey HSD test results (groupings of means)

methods.description: Laboratory procedure for preparing soil samples to isolate clean diatom frustules suitable for microscopic observation and identification.

steps.description: Soil samples for diatom analysis were taken from a layer of 0-5 cm...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect soil samples specifically for diatom analysis from 0-5 cm depth

steps.description: ...and transferred to Petri dishes.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer soil samples to Petri dishes for initial handling

steps.description: The samples were digested in a mixture of concentrated sulfuric acid (H 2 SO 4 ) and potassium dichromate (K 2 Cr 2 O 7 ) to obtain clean diatom frustules.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest soil samples using a sulfochromic mixture (H₂SO₄ + K₂Cr₂O₇) to remove organic matter and clean diatom frustules

steps.description: To remove the sulfochromic mixture, the material was centrifuged at 2500 rpm with distilled water several times until the chromic mixture was completely removed.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash digested material by repeated centrifugation with distilled water to remove acid mixture

steps.description: Excess inorganic matter within the soil was removed by sedimentation.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove excess inorganic matter from the sample by sedimentation

steps.description: Cleaned diatom valves were placed onto coverslips...
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer cleaned diatom frustules onto coverslips for slide preparation

steps.description: ...and left to dry.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry coverslips containing diatom frustules

steps.description: When the material was completely dry, the coverslips with diatoms were mounted in synthetic Pleurax resin, ZBE Kraków, Poland (refractive index: 1.75).
steps.searchable_summary: Mount dried coverslips with diatoms onto microscope slides using Pleurax resin

methods.description: Procedure for observing, identifying, and counting diatom species on prepared microscope slides using light microscopy.

steps.description: Diatoms were identified and counted under an LM Carl Zeiss Axio Imager A2 equipped with a 100× Plan Apochromatic objective with differential interference contrast (DIC) for oil immersion.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe diatoms on slides using a light microscope with specific objective and DIC optics under oil immersion
inputs: LM Carl Zeiss Axio Imager A2 microscope

steps.description: Diatom images were captured with a Zeiss AxioCam ICc5 camera (Jena, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Capture images of observed diatoms using a microscope camera

steps.description: They were counted to 400 valves by scanning transects across the coverslip.
steps.searchable_summary: Count diatom valves by scanning transects across the coverslip until a minimum count (400 valves) is reached

steps.description: The identification of diatom species was based on studies conducted by Krammer [26], Hofmann et al. [14] and Levkov et al. [27].
steps.searchable_summary: Identify diatom species based on morphological characteristics using specified taxonomic reference literature
outputs: Identified diatom species list per sample
